mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% over 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment declines without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal a tightening cycle that compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict SPX returns with Rank IC > 0.05, or negative returns occur without the dual signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) above 1,300k units signal robust demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through increased construction activity and consumer confidence.
failure_condition: SPX fails to generate positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1,300k units, or housing data decouples from equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 103.0 signals stronger manufacturing activity, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window, or positive industrial data coincides with negative equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) below 730,000m dollars signals weak demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where RSAFS declines by >1.5%, or spending data decouples from equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a strong labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved consumer spending.
failure_condition: Interaction between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03), or positive labor data coincides with negative equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle characterized by rising FEDFUNDS above 4.0% and stagnant CPIAUCSL signals reduced liquidity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03), or negative returns occur without the dual signal.
